title: Coach
subtitle: Evidence-based, deterministic training guidance personalized from your own metrics — offline, with every recommendation traceable to a source.
disclaimer: Educational, not medical advice. Plans assume an apparently healthy adult cleared for exercise. Consult a qualified professional before starting, and stop and seek care for chest pain, fainting, or unusual breathlessness.
programs_note: One worked reference program (intermediate marathon, 16 weeks) is included to demonstrate the pattern. The full goal × sport matrix is generated block-by-block against this verified backbone (see coverage).

approach.detail: Pyramidal (~80% easy / 15% threshold / 5% hard) by default, with polarized and threshold models also stored so a block can shift emphasis.
Meso/microcycles on a 3:1 loading pattern with a deload every 4th week; pre-race taper cuts volume ~50% while holding intensity and frequency.
Heavy/explosive resistance training improves economy and maximal strength without harming endurance; separated from quality sessions and tapered out before racing.
Weekly load increases capped (~8–10%) with scheduled deloads — justified by progressive-overload prudence, not by ACWR (see safety).

reference_program.entry_gate: Recent average ≥35 km/week or CTL ≥40 (otherwise routed to base-building).
reference_program.taper: Final ~2 weeks cut volume ~50% while maintaining intensity and frequency.

personalization.summary: Twelve closed-form rules map your metrics to concrete targets, with a fallback when an input is missing. No runtime model.

worked_example.detail: Tanaka 208 − 0.7×age (≈181 bpm at 38); a measured value overrides; large standard error flagged.
Heart-rate reserve span between resting and max HR.
Computed from your VO2-cost relationship (no copyrighted table); updates as vo2max_vdot changes.
Recent weekly average becomes the start; increases capped ~8–10% with deloads.
≥35 km/week or CTL ≥40 for the marathon plan.
TSB band guides readiness; two-week taper cuts volume ~50%, holds intensity.
If aerobic_decoupling exceeds ~5% on a long run, hold rather than extend.

normative_tables.name: HRmax prediction equations (Fox, Tanaka, Gulati) + accuracy/SE
Protein intake by goal and per dose
Strength training effects in endurance athletes

safety.red_s: Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport is a first-class constraint: a low-availability threshold near 30 kcal/kg fat-free mass/day triggers education and referral, not aggressive prescription. Weight-loss content is capped at a sustainable rate and paired with elevated protein to protect lean mass.
safety.anti_doping: Content stays within nutrition, training and recovery; defers to current WADA guidance; nothing touching prohibited methods or substances.
safety.acwr: The acute-to-chronic workload ratio (proposed lower-risk ~0.8–1.3) has been substantively criticized for mathematical coupling and collider bias. The app treats a large acute spike as a prompt for caution but presents no ACWR number as a validated injury predictor.

coverage.cell: Cycling — criterium, 40k TT, gran fondo, ultra, MTB/gravel
Cross-sport — get strong, lose weight, stay fit

glossary.def: Drift of HR relative to pace/power over a steady effort; rising decoupling signals fading aerobic durability.
Acute training load; short-term EWMA of load; fatigue proxy.
Chronic training load; long-term EWMA of load; fitness proxy.
Output (pace/power) ÷ heart rate; tracks aerobic efficiency over time.
Functional threshold power; anchor for cycling power zones.
Normalized power ÷ FTP for a session.
Zone method using the span between resting and maximal heart rate.
Physiologically weighted average power accounting for variability.
The three intensity-distribution models the app stores.
Training stress balance; CTL − ATL; a form proxy for readiness and taper timing.
VO2-based running fitness index used to derive paces by computation.
Normalized power ÷ average power; how evenly an effort was paced.
